sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Six Flags Entertainment (FUN) PT Lowered to $29 at Guggenheim
feed.summary: Guggenheim analyst Curry Baker has lowered the price target for Six Flags Entertainment (FUN) to $29. This article is a premium only piece, requiring a StreetInsider.com Premium subscription to access the full details. It indicates a revised outlook from Guggenheim regarding the valuation of Six Flags Entertainment.

feed.title: Albemarle (ALB) Surges 5.1% Amid Market Volatility
feed.summary: Albemarle (ALB) stock surged 5.1% despite a broader market decline, driven by rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions. The company, a major lithium producer, benefits from its essential role in the EV battery market. However, its high price-to-sales ratio and recent insider selling suggest potential overvaluation and caution for investors.

feed.title: Iran war: What’s driving market sentiment?
feed.summary: Markets have shown resilience despite the ongoing conflict in Iran, with the S&P 500 Index remaining nearly flat after six weeks, credit spreads tightening, and volatility receding. This suggests that investors are not pricing in an endlessly spiraling conflict but rather seeking narrative closure and anticipating an eventual end. The broader macro backdrop, including resilient global leading indicators and contained inflation expectations, also continues to support risk assets.

feed.title: Stryker agrees to acquire IVL developer Amplitude Vascular Systems
feed.summary: Stryker has announced its agreement to acquire Amplitude Vascular Systems (AVS), marking its entry into the rapidly growing intravascular lithotripsy (IVL) market. AVS's Pulse IVL system, designed to treat calcified peripheral artery disease, is currently undergoing pivotal trials. This acquisition follows other major medtech players like Johnson & Johnson and Boston Scientific moving into the IVL space, highlighting the market's increasing significance.

feed.title: Former SoCalGas CEO Maryam Brown takes over Indiana Michigan Power
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) has appointed Maryam S. Brown as president and chief operating officer of Indiana Michigan Power (I&M), effective April 27, 2026. Brown previously served as president and CEO of Southern California Gas Company and brings over 25 years of utility, regulatory, and policy experience. She will succeed Steve Baker, who will transition to a senior advisor role before retiring at the end of 2026.

feed.title: AEP Names Maryam S. Brown President and COO of Indiana Michigan Power
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) has appointed Maryam S. Brown as the new president and chief operating officer of Indiana Michigan Power (I&M), effective April 27. Brown, previously CEO of Southern California Gas Company, brings over 25 years of experience in utility operations and financial management, and will report to AEP chairman, president, and CEO Bill Fehrman. She succeeds Steve Baker, who will serve as a senior advisor for the transition and AEP's nuclear development strategy in Indiana until his retirement at the end of 2026.

feed.title: Qualcomm expands strategic advanced driver assistance systems, immersive eyewear collaborations
feed.summary: Qualcomm Technologies is expanding its partnerships with Bosch and Snap Inc.'s Specs Inc. to advance both advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) for digital cockpits and immersive eyewear. The collaboration with Bosch focuses on scaling intelligent vehicle technology with Snapdragon Ride platforms, while the partnership with Specs Inc. aims to enhance future generations of immersive eyewear using Snapdragon system-on-a-chip technology, building on existing XR services collaboration. Both initiatives target increased functionality, safety, and user experience in their respective domains.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams leases warehouse and office space in South Portland
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams has leased 13,100 square feet of warehouse and office space in South Portland at 2325 Broadway. The location is considered ideal due to its proximity to I-295, the Maine Mall, and the Portland International Jetport. Malone Commercial Brokers facilitated the deal, highlighting the continued strength of the Greater Portland commercial real estate market.

feed.title: Conagra Brands Names J.M. Smucker Executive John Brase as CEO
feed.summary: Conagra Brands has appointed John Brase, formerly President and COO of The J.M. Smucker Co., as its new President and CEO, effective June 1. Brase succeeds Sean Connolly, who led Conagra for over a decade, overseeing its transformation into a pure-play branded food company, including the acquisition of Pinnacle Foods. Brase brings extensive experience from both J.M. Smucker and Procter & Gamble, with a strong record in brand building and performance.

feed.title: Iron Mountain tops out Chennai data center, India
feed.summary: Iron Mountain has announced the topping out of its new CHN-1 data center in Chennai, India. This facility will provide 23.2MW of power capacity in its first building, with the entire two-building campus eventually offering 42MW. The move signifies Iron Mountain's continued expansion in India, building on its 2021 entry and subsequent acquisition of Web Werks.

feed.title: Auto‑Callable Contingent‑Interest Notes (AMJB) — 6.0% Daily Fee, Up to 85% Principal Risk
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC is offering Auto Callable Contingent Interest Notes (AMJB) linked to the MerQube US Tech+ Vol Advantage Index, guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. These notes offer contingent monthly interest payments if the Index is at or above 75.00% of its Initial Value, but expose investors to potential principal losses of up to 85.00%. A significant risk factor is the 6.0% per annum daily deduction and a notional financing cost applied to the underlying index, which can materially reduce performance and are explicitly identified as drags on the investment. The notes may be automatically called starting October 27, 2026.

feed.title: Citigroup Adjusts Seagate Technology PT to $595 From $480, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Seagate Technology (STX) to $595 from $480, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an increased positive outlook for the data storage systems manufacturer. The article also notes other recent analyst adjustments for Seagate, with price targets ranging from $485 to $620 from firms like Susquehanna, Mizuho, Bernstein, William O'Neil, and Evercore ISI.

feed.title: Baird Adjusts Price Target on CoStar Group to $56 From $73, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Baird has adjusted its price target for CoStar Group (CSGP) shares, lowering it to $56 from the previous $73. Despite this reduction, the firm has decided to maintain its "Outperform" rating on the stock. The article, published by MT Newswires, details this analyst recommendation along with other recent news concerning CoStar Group.

feed.title: Baird Adjusts Price Target on Jacobs Solutions to $126 From $130, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Baird has adjusted its price target for Jacobs Solutions Inc. (J) to $126, down from the previous $130, while continuing to maintain a Neutral rating on the stock. This update in the price target was reported on April 13, 2026, by MT Newswires.

feed.title: T-Mobile gains after KeyBanc upgrade highlights growth outlook
feed.summary: T-Mobile (TMUS) shares are gaining after KeyBanc upgraded the stock to Overweight from Sector Weight, citing strong growth outlook. The analyst highlighted T-Mobile's significant capital return program, expected to exceed $60 billion by the end of 2027, which is perceived as undervalued by the market.

feed.title: Gladstone Investment Corporation (NASDAQ:GAIN) Sees Large Drop in Short Interest
feed.summary: Gladstone Investment Corporation (NASDAQ:GAIN) experienced a significant 41.3% decrease in short interest, falling to 1,339,262 shares as of March 31st, indicating a reduction in bearish sentiment. Despite this, analyst sentiment remains cautious with an average "Hold" rating, and the company recently missed quarterly EPS expectations. Gladstone offers an attractive monthly dividend yield of 6.5%, though its stock performance has shown modest trading activity.

feed.title: Mizuho Adjusts Price Target on Everest Group to $365 From $360, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Mizuho has adjusted its price target for Everest Group (EG) to $365, an increase from the previous $360, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This update reflects a modest change in the analyst's valuation perspective for the reinsurance company. The article also provides a brief overview of Everest Group's business activities, including its reinsurance and insurance segments.

feed.title: Fastenal shares slide as Q1 results meet expectations but margin pressure and limited upside weigh on sentiment
feed.summary: Fastenal Company (FAST) shares fell 3.9% after releasing first-quarter results that met expectations but highlighted ongoing gross-margin pressure and a conservative outlook. Despite solid sales growth and improved operating leverage, unfavorable price/cost dynamics and other headwinds are limiting the stock's near-term upside, especially for a company that often trades at a premium valuation. The company reported Q1 net sales of approximately $2.20 billion and diluted EPS of $0.30, but gross margin declined to 44.6% from 45.1% due to price/cost issues, transportation costs, and customer rebates.

feed.title: Former Smucker COO picked as new Conagra CEO
feed.summary: John Brase, the former Chief Operating Officer of Smucker, has been named the new Chief Executive Officer of Conagra. The announcement was made on April 13, 2026, by Bloomberg via Crain's Cleveland Business.

feed.title: Your Customer Advocates Are Not Your Crisis Communications Plan
feed.summary: Turning to loyal customers as the primary defense during a company crisis is not a responsible communications strategy, despite their potential value. While advocates can share positive experiences and extend credibility, their praise cannot overshadow genuine problems or serious issues. Companies should instead activate a dedicated crisis team, focusing on swift, accurate, and empathetic responses, and allow loyal customers to assist on their own terms by keeping them informed and seeking their counsel.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of Gemini Space Station, Inc. Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit on behalf of investors in Gemini Space Station, Inc. (NASDAQ:GEMI). The lawsuit alleges that Gemini Space, a crypto platform, made false and misleading statements regarding its business viability and international expansion during its IPO in September 2025 and throughout the class period. Investors have until May 18, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion, and the firm encourages affected investors to contact them for a complimentary case evaluation.

feed.title: CCPA Final Regulations: Business Compliance Guide
feed.summary: The CCPA Final Regulations introduce significant new obligations for businesses to protect consumer data, effective January 1, 2026. Key changes include mandatory annual cybersecurity audits, comprehensive risk assessments for high-risk data processing, and enhanced governance requirements for Automated Decision-Making Technology (ADMT). This guide outlines these new provisions, their applicability criteria, implementation timelines, and ongoing compliance obligations, stressing the importance of proactive preparation to avoid substantial penalties.

feed.title: How Jabil uses AI and robotics to bolster its decarbonization agenda
feed.summary: Jabil, a contract manufacturer for major companies like Apple and Amazon, is using AI and robotics to enhance its decarbonization efforts. The company invested $500 million in 2025 to overhaul its factory systems, leading to a 47% reduction in operational emissions since 2019, putting it on track to halve emissions by 2030. Jabil also leverages employee-led innovation and acquisitions to create new revenue streams from materials recovery and equipment refurbishment, demonstrating a successful integration of sustainability with business growth.

feed.title: Chipotle Opens New Restaurant in Melbourne
feed.summary: Chipotle is expanding its presence in Brevard County, Florida, with a new restaurant opening in Melbourne on April 14, 2026. This location will be the ninth Chipotle in the Space Coast area and will feature a convenient drive-thru pickup lane. The expansion highlights the growing demand for Chipotle's customizable Mexican-inspired cuisine and the company's focus on consumer convenience.

feed.title: $195.30–$203.80 range contains Bank of Montreal stock flat movement
feed.summary: Bank of Montreal (BMO) stock is currently in a strong bullish trend despite recent selling pressure, trading above key moving averages. The bank's initiatives into AI and quantum computing are ongoing, though not yet a significant catalyst for sustained upside. While short-term momentum signals are mixed and show overbought conditions, analysts predict a high probability of further price increases within the C$195.30 to C$203.80 range for the next five trading days, with C$195 acting as crucial short-term support.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: U.S.-Based Diversified Energy to Ring Bell for 25 Years Since its Founding
feed.summary: The New York Stock Exchange announced that Diversified Energy (NYSE: DEC) will celebrate its 25th anniversary of founding by ringing the closing bell. The company's CEO, Rusty Hutson, will discuss why the NYSE is their premier listing venue. The update also covers other market insights, including early Monday market slides and Robert Herjavec discussing cyber threats.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Adjusts Price Target on Simon Property Group to $198 From $200, Maintains In Line Rating
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has adjusted its price target for Simon Property Group (SPG) to $198, down from $200, while reiterating an "In Line" rating on the stock. This update reflects a minor revision in the analyst's valuation of the real estate investment trust. The article also notes a separate adjustment by Barclays, which raised its price target for SPG to $201 from $193, maintaining an "Equalweight" rating.

feed.title: Guggenheim Cuts Six Flags Entertainment (NYSE:FUN) Price Target to $29.00
feed.summary: Guggenheim has reduced its price target for Six Flags Entertainment (NYSE:FUN) from $33.00 to $29.00 but maintained a "buy" rating, indicating a potential upside of 51.44%. This comes after Six Flags missed its quarterly EPS estimates, posting ($0.91) against an expected ($0.31), despite exceeding revenue forecasts with $650.1 million. The stock currently trades around $19.15, and analysts collectively give it a "Hold" rating with a consensus target of $23.69.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Lowers S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) Price Target to $525.00
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has reduced its price target for S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) to $525.00 from $530.00, while maintaining an "overweight" rating. Despite the lowered target, analyst sentiment remains largely positive, with a consensus "Buy" rating and an average price target of $558.47 across multiple firms. S&P Global recently traded at $422.06, has a market cap of $128.1 billion, and reported quarterly EPS of $4.30, slightly missing consensus estimates.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) Stock Drops as Analyst Cuts Rating on Growth Concerns
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) shares dropped over 3% after Raymond James downgraded the stock from "Strong Buy" to "Outperform," citing reduced conviction in near-term growth prospects and cutting the price objective to $29 from $30. The downgrade stems from HPE's Cloud & AI division underperforming due to a strategic focus on profitability over volume, and challenges within its networking business including integration issues from the Juniper acquisition. Despite the downgrade, Raymond James still views HPE as undervalued, predicting modest mid-single-digit revenue growth.

feed.title: Regeneron Enters $2.1 Billion Radiopharma Collaboration with Telix Pharmaceuticals
feed.summary: Regeneron and Telix Pharmaceuticals have formed a collaboration valued at up to $2.1 billion to develop next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies for precision oncology. The partnership leverages Regeneron's antibody discovery platform and Telix's radiopharmaceutical development capabilities, including isotope selection, manufacturing, and global distribution. The initial focus is on multiple solid tumor targets, with co-developed diagnostic agents for patient selection and treatment monitoring.

feed.title: Portnoy Law Firm Announces Class Action on Behalf of Boston Scientific Corporation Investors
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit on behalf of Boston Scientific Corporation (NYSE: BSX) investors who purchased securities between July 3, 2025, and February 3, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Boston Scientific failed to disclose competitive pressures affecting its U.S. Electrophysiology market share, leading to a significant stock price drop after its Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings missed guidance. Investors have until May 4, 2026, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

feed.title: Labor secretary climbs Union Pacific's Big Boy as 61,000 visit
feed.summary: U.S. Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer joined Union Pacific CEO Jim Vena for a tour of Big Boy No. 4014 and the J.R. David Yard in Roseville, California, as part of the locomotive's coast-to-coast tour celebrating America's 250th birthday. The event drew an estimated 61,000 visitors and highlighted the railroad's historical significance in connecting the nation and driving economic growth. Big Boy No. 4014, the world's largest operating steam locomotive, commenced its tour from Sacramento, commemorating the first transcontinental railroad's origins.

feed.title: Small Molecule Innovator API CDMO Market Outlook, 2026-2033 - Lonza Group, Novo Holdings (Catalent) and Thermo Fisher Scientific Lead the Growing Industry
feed.summary: The global small molecule innovator API CDMO market is projected to grow significantly from $26.66 billion in 2025 to $43.75 billion by 2033, driven by increased demand for small molecule drugs, outsourcing trends in pharmaceuticals, and a surge in clinical trials. Key players like Lonza Group, Novo Holdings (Catalent), and Thermo Fisher Scientific are leading this growth. The market expansion is also supported by the focus on specialty medicines and the critical need for cost-effective, efficient API production amidst complex regulations.

feed.title: Stryker to Acquire Amplitude Vascular Systems
feed.summary: Stryker Corp. is acquiring Amplitude Vascular Systems Inc. (AVS), a medical technology firm specializing in a next-generation intravascular lithotripsy (IVL) platform for calcified peripheral arterial disease. This acquisition will enhance Stryker's Peripheral Vascular portfolio with revascularization technology, complementing its existing offerings and expanding its market presence in arterial disease treatment. The deal aims to provide physicians with expanded treatment options and improve care for patients with calcified peripheral arterial disease.

feed.title: STERIS plc stock (US8589121081): Is its infection control dominance strong enough to unlock steady h
feed.summary: STERIS plc (NYSE: STE), an Irish-domiciled company listed on Nasdaq, is presented as a defensive investment opportunity in the healthcare sector, focusing on infection prevention and sterilization solutions. The company's business model combines hardware sales with recurring service contracts, which account for over half of its revenue, providing stable income and strong margins. STERIS's dominance in a highly regulated market, especially in the US, positions it to benefit from increasing healthcare spending and patient safety priorities, while strategically utilizing bolt-on acquisitions and digital transformation to maintain its competitive edge.

feed.title: How Renewed Analyst Optimism And Rising Institutional Ownership At Citizens Financial Group (CFG) Has Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group is experiencing renewed analyst optimism and increasing institutional ownership, with Altfest L J & Co. Inc. significantly boosting its stake in the bank. These developments precede the bank's upcoming earnings report, highlighting growing confidence among professional investors in Citizens' business strategies. Despite this, the article cautions that potential risks from commercial real estate exposure could still impact credit quality and profitability, urging investors to consider various scenarios.

feed.title: High Temperature Adhesives Market size to surpass $10.6 Billion
feed.summary: The high-temperature adhesives market, valued at $5.29 billion in 2025, is projected to reach $10.6 billion by 2035 with a CAGR of 7.2%. This growth is driven by industrial development, the adoption of advanced materials, and the increasing need for high-performance bonding solutions in industries like automotive and electronics, particularly in the Asia Pacific region. Key trends include demand for lightweight materials, the expansion of electric vehicles and renewable energy systems, and advancements in adhesive technologies, with major players like 3M, Henkel, and Dow leading innovation.

feed.title: BMO Capital Markets Raises DuPont de Nemours Price Target to $60
feed.summary: BMO Capital Markets has increased its price target for DuPont de Nemours (NYSE: DD) from $25.10 to $60.00, maintaining an "outperform" rating. This new target represents a potential upside of 27.33% from the previous close, reflecting the company's strong fundamentals and growth potential. Other analysts have also raised their price targets, indicating growing confidence in DuPont's performance within the specialty materials, chemicals, and industrial sectors.

feed.title: Stable session for Energy Transfer stock as $19.05 support draws focus
feed.summary: Energy Transfer LP (ET) is trading at $19.12, showing a generally bullish trend despite a mild 0.36% decline today. The stock is holding above its key moving averages and maintaining bullish technical indicators, with strong buyer domination and low intraday volatility. Experts suggest that ET is expected to consolidate between $19.05 support and $19.60 resistance, with a high probability of an upside move if the $19.05 support holds.

feed.title: Autodesk Stock Faces Downgrade As Citi Warns Of Near Term Software Sector Pullback
feed.summary: Autodesk (ADSK) stock was downgraded by Citi analyst Tyler Radke from Buy to Neutral, with the price target cut to $246. This downgrade reflects a broader cautious outlook on the software sector rather than specific concerns about Autodesk's fundamentals, though the company faces near-term risks like a sales restructuring and a premium valuation. Despite Citi's bearish stance, the majority of analysts remain bullish on ADSK, with a consensus price target suggesting significant upside.

feed.title: Fenbo Holdings (FEBO) director Dai Lei submits initial insider Form 3
feed.summary: Fenbo Holdings (FEBO) director Dai Lei has submitted an initial Form 3, a mandatory statement of beneficial ownership for insiders, but the filing does not report any current share holdings or recent transactions. This initial filing serves to disclose Dai Lei's position as a director without indicating any specific trading activity or derivative positions. Investors can use this filing as a baseline to track future insider trading actions.

feed.title: Regeneron, Telix Enter Strategic Radiopharma Collaboration
feed.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals and Telix Pharmaceuticals Ltd. have announced a strategic collaboration to jointly develop and commercialize next-generation radiopharmaceutical therapies. This partnership will combine Regeneron's biologics expertise, including bispecific antibody discovery, with Telix's radiopharmaceutical development platform and manufacturing capabilities. The collaboration aims to address solid tumor targets and develop radio-diagnostics, with Telix receiving an upfront payment of $40 million for initial programs and potential milestones and royalties.

feed.title: Dollar General to offer AI-enabled audio with targeted ads in select stores
feed.summary: Dollar General is expanding its in-store audio network by partnering with Qsic to deploy an AI-enabled audio network across approximately 6,000 stores, doubling its existing presence. This initiative, part of the DG Media Network, aims to deliver localized, data-driven audio content and targeted ads to customers and brand partners. The retailer is also leveraging other collaborations with Fetch, Kevel, Recess, and Criteo to enhance its retail media capabilities and provide advanced measurement to advertisers.

feed.title: Georgia Power reminds customers to dig safely this spring
feed.summary: Georgia Power is reminding customers to prioritize safety during spring planting and home improvement projects by calling 811 before digging. This precaution helps prevent power outages, service disruptions, and safety risks by ensuring underground utilities are marked. Additionally, Georgia Power encourages strategic tree planting to improve energy efficiency and avoid future maintenance issues with power lines.

feed.title: The Infrastructure Giant: A Comprehensive 2026 Research Feature on Amazon.com (AMZN)
feed.summary: This 2026 research feature highlights Amazon's evolution from an e-commerce giant to a global infrastructure provider, emphasizing its crucial role in the AI arms race and its new venture into space-based telecommunications with Amazon Leo. The article details Amazon's diversified business model, strong financial performance, and leadership under Andy Jassy, while also addressing competitive pressures, regulatory risks like the FTC antitrust trial, and future growth opportunities.

feed.title: TD Cowen cuts Guardant Health stock price target on valuation By Investing.com
feed.summary: TD Cowen has reduced its price target for Guardant Health (NASDAQ:GH) shares from $135 to $120, while maintaining a Buy rating, citing valuation concerns due to higher interest rates and a volatile macroeconomic environment. Despite the stock trading at $79.55, suggesting an upside to the new target, InvestingPro analysis indicates it might be currently overvalued. The firm anticipates first-quarter sales of $306 million and projects key catalysts such as upcoming FDA decisions for G360 and Shield ACS, along with MolDX reimbursement decisions for Reveal.

feed.title: SailPoint recognized as a 2026 Gartner® Peer Insights™ Customers’ Choice for Identity Governance and Administration
feed.summary: SailPoint has been recognized as a Customers’ Choice in the 2026 Gartner Peer Insights “Voice of the Customer for Identity Governance and Administration” report. The company received an overall rating of 4.8 out of 5, with 96% of respondents willing to recommend SailPoint. This recognition highlights SailPoint's strong customer satisfaction and strategic initiatives in identity security, including securing AI and managing non-human identities.

feed.title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise stock down 3% as slowing growth worries analysts
feed.summary: Shares of Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) fell over 3% after Raymond James downgraded the stock from "Strong Buy" to "Outperform," citing less certainty around growth prospects. Analysts noted that HPE's Cloud & AI segment has underperformed, and its focus on profitability over market share in AI has limited its participation in large-scale projects. Despite a reduced price target and modest revenue growth expectations, analysts still consider HPE an attractive value stock with potential long-term upside.

feed.title: 3 Oversold Healthcare Stocks to Buy After Jobs Data
feed.summary: The March jobs report showing significant growth in the healthcare sector creates an opportunity for investors to consider oversold healthcare stocks. HCA Healthcare and Tenet Healthcare demonstrate strong operational performance and institutional support, while Universal Health Services presents a turnaround opportunity with its acquisition of Talkspace. These companies benefit from consistent demand for medical care, making them attractive defensive plays.
